summary: Escalating tensions between the US and Iran dominate global headlines, with military operations creating severe economic consequences including millions of job losses, while the Trump administration signals skepticism toward Iranian peace proposals and reviews a 14-point plan. The conflict is reshaping regional dynamics, prompting US arms sales to Middle East allies, a German warning about potential US troop withdrawals, and unexpected spillover effects including strengthened Ukrainian positioning and disruptions to global supply chains from blocked sea routes and airline collapses. Secondary crises range from humanitarian emergencies in Brazil and Sudan to environmental concerns from Philippine volcanic ash and Irish wildfires, while domestic issues in the US include economic scrutiny, political violence concerns, and a Supreme Court abortion case. Broader instability appears concentrated across the Middle East and Eastern Europe, with Israeli-Palestinian tensions persisting despite ceasefire claims in Lebanon and Gaza.

articles.title: Millions of jobs lost as Iranians battle ‘Operation Economic Fury’
articles.desc: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is crippling life in Tehran.
